title: Apparatus and methods for user device buffer management in wireless networks
abstract: Apparatus and methods for improving throughput and reliability in a wireless network. In one embodiment, the apparatus and methods provide mechanisms for wireless user device buffer management that mitigate buffer overflow within the device due to overscheduling, such as from different networks with which the device is connected simultaneously. In one variant, a 3GPP-based signaling architecture from wireless device to the multiple networks is provided to enable user device-controlled management buffer overflow. In another variant, potentially buffer-demanding (e.g., HARQ) process management and prioritization rules are defined to avoid buffer overflow. In other variant, buffer size computation is provided considering the number of networks to which the wireless user device can connect.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of data processing of a user device connected to two or more wireless access nodes, the method comprising: monitoring scheduled data activity relating to two or more data connections between the user device and respective ones of the two or more wireless access nodes; determining that the user device is overscheduled; and based at least on the determining, causing the user device to send a delay request to delay one or more buffer-consumptive processes for at least one of the two or more connections. 2. The method of Claim 1, wherein the delay request to delay is configured based at least on a priority of each of the two or more connections. 3. The method of Claim 1, further comprising processing one or more buffer-consumptive processes for a non-delayed one of the two or more connections to completion before processing the delayed one or more buffer-consumptive processes. 4. The method of Claim 1, further comprising processing one or more buffer-consumptive processes for a non-delayed one of the two or more connections to a point of partial completion before processing the delayed one or more buffer- consumptive processes, the point of partial completion comprising a point wherein processing of both of the delayed and non-delayed processes will not overflow a designated buffer of the user device. 5. The method of Claim 4, wherein the one or more wireless access nodes each comprise 3GPP (3rd Generation Partnership Project) gNodeB devices, and the non-delayed one or more buffer-consumptive processes each comprise a HARQ (hybrid automatic retransmission request) process. 6. The method of Claim 5, wherein the causing of the user device to send the delay request comprises sending an information element (IE) to at least one of the gNodeB devices. 7. A method of connecting a wireless user device to multiple wireless networks, the method comprising: evaluating a scheduling load for the user device with respect to each of the multiple networks; based at least on the evaluating, computing a respective allocation for each one 

of the multiple networks, the respective allocation indicating a portion of a prescribed value to be scheduled by the respective one of the multiple networks; based at least on the respective allocation for each one of the multiple networks, allocating a portion of a data buffer of the user device to each one of the multiple networks; configuring information elements (IEs) for use in resource control communications with respective ones of the multiple networks, the respective IEs including the respective allocation for the ones of the multiple networks; and transmitting the IEs to respective ones of the multiple networks to alter a scheduling behavior thereof. 8. The method of Claim 7, wherein: at least a portion of the multiple networks comprise 3 GPP (Third Generation Partnership Project) 5G NR (Fifth Generation New Radio) compliant networks; and the configuring information elements (IEs) for use in resource control communications comprises configuring IEs for use in RRC (Radio Resource Control) channel communications of the at least portion of the multiple networks. 9. The method of Claim 8, wherein: the user device comprises a 3GPP-compliant UE (user equipment); and at least the acts of evaluating and computing are performed by the 3GPP- compliant UE. 10. The method of Claim 8, wherein the configuring information elements (IEs) comprises configuring one or more RLC (Radio Link Control)-parameters Information Elements (IEs) to include a buffer scaling parameter, the buffer scaling parameter related to the prescribed value. 11. The method of Claim 7, wherein the configuring one or more RLC (Radio Link Control)-parameters Information Elements (IEs) to include a buffer scaling parameter comprises configuring the one or more RLC-parameters IEs to include Layer 2 (L2) buffer scaling parameter data. 12. The method of Claim 11, wherein the L2 buffer scaling parameter data comprises fractional or numerical scaling factor data relative to an established maximum buffer size value. 13. The method of Claim 8, further comprising receiving, after and responsive to the transmitting, communications from at least one of the multiple networks 

indicating at least one new or modified scheduling behavior. 14. The method of Claim 13, wherein the receiving, after and responsive to the transmitting, communications from at least one of the multiple networks indicating at least one new or modified scheduling behavior, comprises receiving at least one communication relating to two or more of the multiple networks. 15. The method of Claim 7, wherein the transmitting the IEs to respective ones of the multiple networks to alter a scheduling behavior thereof comprises transmitting at least two of the IEs to a single one of the multiple networks, the single one of the multiple networks configured to forward an IE for a second one of the multiple networks to an entity of the second network. 16. Computer readable apparatus configured for use in a wireless user device, the computer readable apparatus comprising at least one computer program having a plurality of instructions configured to, when executed on a processing apparatus of the wireless user device, estimate buffer size for the wireless user device based on connectivity via at least: determination of whether a multi-connectivity mode of the wireless user device is to be used; based at least on the determination indicating that the multi-connectivity mode is not to be used, causation of a computation of a first value, and multiplication of the first value by a number related to a SIM (subscriber identity module) configuration of the user device to compute an estimated buffer size; and based at least on the determination indicating that the multi-connectivity mode is to be used: cause a computation of a second value, and multiplication of the second value by a number related to a SIM (subscriber identity module) configuration of the user device to compute an estimated buffer size, to produce a third value; cause a computation of a fourth value, and multiplication of the second value by a number related to a SIM (subscriber identity module) configuration of the user device to compute an estimated buffer size, to produce a fifth value; and cause a computation of the buffer size as the larger of the third or the fifth values. 17. The computer readable apparatus of Claim 16, wherein the number related to a SIM configuration comprises a number of respective PLMNs to which the user device can connect simultaneously via respective SIM credentials. 18. The computer readable apparatus of Claim 17, wherein the estimated buffer size comprises an estimated 3GPP Layer 2 (L2) buffer size. 19. The computer readable apparatus of Claim 18, wherein the second value comprises: [MaxULDataRate MN * RLCRTT MN + MaxULDataRate SN * RLCRTT SN + MaxDLDataRate SN * V ALUE2=RLCRTT_SN + MaxDLDataRate MN * (RLCRTT SN + X2/Xn delay + Queuing in SN)] and the fourth value comprises: [MaxULDataRate MN * RLCRTT MN + MaxULDataRate SN * RLCRTT SN + MaxDLDataRate MN * RLCRTT MN + MaxDLDataRate SN * (RLCRTT MN + X2/Xn delay + Queuing in MN)] and wherein: MaxDLDataRate comprises Maximum DL (downlink) data rate; MaxDLDataRate_MN comprises Maximum DL data rate in the MN (master node); MaxDLDataRate_SN comprises Maximum DL data rate in the SN (secondary node); MaxULDataRate comprises Maximum UL (uplink) data rate; X2/Xn delay + Queuing in SN (secondary node) comprises 25ms if SCG is NR, and 55ms if SCG is EUTRA; X2/Xn delay + Queuing in MN (master node) comprises 25ms if MCG is NR, and 55ms if MCG is EUTRA; RLC RTT for EUTRA cell group comprises 75ms; and RLC RTT for NR cell group ranges between 20 ms and 50 ms. 20. A method of connecting a wireless user device to at least two wireless networks, the method comprising: determining a first scheduling load for the user device with respect to a first of the at least two wireless networks; determining a second scheduling load for the user device with respect to a second of the at least two wireless networks; based at least on the determining the first and second scheduling loads, computing a respective allocation for each one of the at least two networks; based at least on the respective allocation for each one of the multiple networks, causing allocating of a portion of a data buffer of the user device to each one of the at least two wireless networks, the causing comprising configuring information elements (IEs) for use in control communications between with respective ones of the at least two networks and the user device, the respective IEs including the respective allocation for the ones of the at least two wireless networks.
